column1: # Edit values below and commit to Git to update the dashboard.

column2: The Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed on Day 23. Brent settled near $107 after peaking at $119 this week. Vietnam enacted an emergency 20% fuel price increase on March 19; RON95 now at 30,690 VND/L. The VN-Index dropped to 1,648 on Mar 20. The free-market USD/VND rate surged to 27,307 (Chogia.vn Mar 22) — a 4% premium over the official Vietcombank rate. The Govt has deployed 3-5k VND/L from the Price Stabilization Fund but reserves are running low (~2,600 bn VND remaining). PM Chinh has secured 4 million barrels from partner countries.
US launches airstrikes on Iranian nuclear facilities; Iran threatens Strait closure
IRGC Navy deploys mines and fast boats across Hormuz shipping lanes
Ship transits drop to zero; Brent surges past $86/bbl
All major carriers announce Hormuz transit suspension
Vietnam MOIT emergency fuel price adjustment; RON95 to 22340 VND/L. P&I clubs withdraw war-risk coverage.
Vietnam Govt issues Resolution 36 on petroleum price stabilization
EIA confirms Brent at $94/bbl; Israel strikes Iran South Pars gas field
PM Pham Minh Chinh directs fuel price stabilization; Price Stabilization Fund activated. RON95 briefly hits 29120.
Extraordinary adjustment: RON95 pulled back to 25240 (heavy PSF subsidy)
SBV intervenes; VN-Index falls below 1700. Free market USD/VND hits 27096
RON95 surges 20% to 30690 VND/L; diesel +24% to 33420; PM calls partners for oil supply
Brent hits $113.40. Vietnam secures 4M barrels from partners. VN-Index drops to 1648.
Day 22 of crisis. Goldman warns oil may stay triple-digits for years.
Free market USD/VND hits 27307. SBV reference rate unchanged at 25085.
